behaviors.description: Request payload to the external API includes the full valid taxonomy label list.
behaviors.justification: Failure is in deterministic request-building logic at one module boundary (classifier -> API client call args). A fake client capturing payload can reveal missing labels. Rejected Integration because behavior text does not require cross-module state handoff beyond a single call argument contract; rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because no real OS/network/model judgment is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Call classify() with a fixed taxonomy and a spy/fake API client; assert the outbound request contains every taxonomy label exactly as required before the client is invoked.

behaviors.description: Out-of-taxonomy provider label is mapped to "unknown".
behaviors.justification: This is output-normalization/validation logic on a single returned value. A stubbed raw response is sufficient to trigger the real failure. Rejected higher levels because behavior text is about label mapping, not real network/OS or model-quality variance.
behaviors.test_description: Feed the normalization/classification path a stubbed API response label not in taxonomy (e.g., "medical"); assert returned label is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: Normalization is deterministic for identical raw API responses.
behaviors.justification: Pure determinism/idempotence check on normalization function behavior; no external boundary required. Rejected Integration+ because no module interaction beyond local logic is needed by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run normalization multiple times with the same raw provider payload fixture; assert identical final label each time.

behaviors.description: Repeated live API calls for same input remain consistent unless provider response changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on live provider outputs over repeated calls; mocks would hide model/provider variability. This is a real AI-call consistency property, not just wrapper logic. Rejected Workflow because it is repeated single-step classification calls, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Execute N real API classifications for identical document+taxonomy under same model/settings; record raw and final labels. Assert final-label changes only when corresponding provider raw response differs.

behaviors.description: Configured token budget is enforced before dispatch (truncate/reject policy).
behaviors.justification: Real failure typically occurs at wiring boundary: budget config + token estimator + request constructor + dispatch gate. In-process integration with fake provider catches whether dispatch is blocked or payload truncated correctly. Rejected System because no real OS/network/subprocess is required; rejected Agentic because this tests pre-dispatch control logic, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier with real tokenizer/budget config and fake API transport. Use over-budget input; assert behavior follows policy (truncate or reject) and that dispatch behavior matches policy.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for typical input stays within configured budget.
behaviors.justification: The assertion depends on real provider-reported usage from an actual AI API call; stubs can mask provider tokenization/accounting differences. Rejected Integration by behavior text requiring provider-reported usage, not local estimate only. Rejected Workflow because this is a single-call property, not multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real classification calls on representative documents and capture provider usage metadata; assert reported total tokens per call do not exceed configured budget threshold.

behaviors.description: On rate-limit signal, module waits and retries instead of returning immediate error.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is orchestration between retry policy/timer and API client error handling. A deterministic fake provider returning rate-limit then success can expose real retry bugs. Rejected System because real network/OS behavior is not necessary to reproduce retry control-flow; rejected Agentic because correctness does not depend on model judgment/content.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake provider sequence: rate-limit response then success. Verify classifier waits/backoffs per policy, retries expected count, and returns success rather than surfacing the initial error.
